title: The missing link in India’s maternal health story
content: Despite improvements in institutional deliveries, women’s empowerment and maternity programmes, exclusive breastfeeding rates in India have declined. NFHS-6 data point to persistent gaps in postnatal support, workplace protection and maternity benefits. Bindu Shajan Perappadan looks at medical, social and economic factors contributing to the decline

title: Five children among 6 killed, in Mumbai chawl collapse
content: According to the information given by Rajawadi Hospital, a seven-year-old boy died during treatment, while the condition of a 24-year-old man is stable

title: Government issues stern notice to Meta on child sexual abuse material in Instagram ads: sources
content: “MeitY has ordered Instagram to disable all ads and content promoting and facilitating access to CSEAM,” the sources said, adding that the Ministry also demanded a detailed explanation within seven days

title: Long-overdue digital survey for Thammanam–Pullepady Road project gets under way
content: Project aims to construct a 3.7-km-long four-lane road linking MG Road with the Edappally–Vyttila NH 66 Bypass by widening and extending the Thammanam–Pullepady Road

title: 256 criminal cases pending against lawmakers of Kerala
content: Twenty-two cases against lawmakers delayed for more than two years and nine cases for over five years

title: Monsoon disrupts lives across India; Mumbai air traffic hit, Himachal, Kerala, Odisha on alert | Live updates
content: Amid threat of heavy rains, Mumbai civic body orders schools and colleges to remain shut on July 6, 2026

title: AI-powered tools can trace missing persons faster, with more accuracy, say experts
content: They can significantly improve speed and accuracy of investigations, especially when time is critical

title: Four persons held for stealing copper wires at railway yard
content: Railway officials found that copper wires on signalling systems along the track between Arakkonam and Tiruttani and Arakkonam and Katpadi were missing for the past several weeks

title: Police to launch screening of rental properties in Kozhikode under Operation Toofan
content: Move follows intelligence inputs that drug traffickers and repeat offenders are using rented premises to store smuggled narcotics; database of landlords and rental properties in vulnerable areas to be prepared as part of the drive

title: State Highways opens ₹20.81-crore ROB in Tirupattur
content: Originally given its nod in 2020, the new bridge was jointly done by the Southern Railway and the Projects wing of the State Highways

title: Six persons arrested for assaulting man at Tiruvannamalai GH
content: The police said when the ambulance halted at the hospital, the gang dragged an injured Silambarasan from the vehicle and beat him before police rescued him

title: Police seize 3.6 kg of ganja in Villupuram, two held
content: The police said initial inquiry revealed the duo had bought ganja from drug peddlers to sell it to individuals in Villupuram district

title: Heavy Mumbai rains: Three dead, four injured as rain disrupts life in Maharashtra
content: Flight operations affected for an hour at Mumbai airport; holiday declared for schools and colleges in Mumbai and Thane on July 5

title: Over 230 Irula families in Ariyalur find steady income through cashew harvesting initiative
content: The Tribal Welfare Department facilitated financial support for people from the Irula community to participate in public auctions for cashew plantations managed by the Tamil Nadu Forest Plantation Corporation Limited to enable them to directly participate in cashew cultivation and marketing

title: Policeman from Kangpokpi questioned in abduction and killing of six Naga men in Manipur
content: Following public outrage over the killings, the State Government handed over the case to the National Investigation Agency, which is yet to make any arrests

title: 20 lakh names deleted after SIR Phase I in Odisha
content: BJD challenges draft report, saying 27 lakh voters were deleted from electoral roll as against the claimed exclusion of 20 Lakh

title: Amit Shah to launch several projects as Ministry of Cooperation completes five years
content: He will lay the foundation stone for 47 grain storage godowns and launch a centralised core banking platform, and Sahakar Sahyogi, a conversational AI-powered platform for Urban Cooperative Banks

title: BJP chief hits out at SP, Congress over Ram Temple issue
content: No individual would be allowed to hurt the sentiments of devotees, says Nitin Nabin, without explicitly referring to the recent allegations of financial irregularities at the Ram Temple

title: SIT formed to probe ₹100-crore disproportionate assets case against former TMC councillor
content: Police arrested Debraj Chakraborty, husband of former Rajarhat-Gopalpur MLA Aditi Munshi, on Wednesday based on an FIR filed by BJP MLA Tarunjyoti Tewari against the couple

title: Gujarat police reunite missing Maharashtra woman with family
content: The woman, who is mentally ill, was found hundreds of kilometres away from her home; she is among the 314 adults and 100 children traced and reunited with their families by the Amreli police since 2025

title: Former SNDP Yogam leader Mahesan’s family meets Home Minister, seeks fresh probe into his death
content: The family urged the Minister to intensify legal action against the first accused, SNDP Yogam general secretary Vellappally Natesan, and constitute a Special Investigation Team (SIT) to reinvestigate the case

title: We are prepared for 2-D Bills — Delimitation and Dismissal: Jairam Ramesh
content: Congress general secretary (communications) Jairam Ramesh claims that the government does not have the numbers to pass either the Delimitation Bill or the Constitution (130th Amendment) Bill for removal of Ministers

title: First instalment of ₹25,863 crore under VB-GRAM G released
content: Uttar Pradesh received the highest allocation at ₹3,210.76 crore, followed by Andhra Pradesh, Rajasthan, and Tamil Nadu, while Kerala received a considerably smaller allocation of ₹925.33 crore; concerns over centralised planning and high technological barrier persist

title: Pothundy twin murder verdict on July 6
content: Lone accused, Chenthamara of Thiruthampadam, Nenmara, is already serving double life imprisonment for the murder of his neighbour Sajitha, who was hacked to death on August 31, 2019

title: Row over Meghalaya tribal council’s Blinkit entry ban
content: A traditional local body criticises the Khasi Hills Autonomous District Council for denying doorstep delivery service of discounted products to six lakh people to protect the interests of 4,000 shops in and around Shillong

title: Complaint by actor Ansiba Hassan: Court directs registration of FIR against Lakshmipriya, SI
content: Ansiba has accused Lakshmipriya of filing a false complaint against her, and Reshma, SI of women’s cell, of subjecting her to harassment and unlawful detention in the name of ‘verifying’  allegations against her

title: Foundation laid for Adani Group’s defence factory in M.P.’s Shivpuri
content: A missile and propellant manufacturing facility is being set up by the Adani Defence and Aerospace at a cost of ₹2,500 crore

title: EAM Jaishankar meets Qatar PM; reviews bilateral cooperation, regional developments
content: During the ongoing visit, Mr. Jaishankar will meet his counterparts and top leaders of the four Gulf countries to enhance bilateral relations and exchange views on regional developments and issues of mutual interest

title: Krishna district police invoke UAPA Act against YouTuber
content: Police register case following a complaint alleging that the YouTuber made comments propagating CPI-Maoist ideology and gave an inciting speech during the Dalita Samara Shankaravam held at Eluru recently

title: India to host two-day BRICS anti-drugs meet
content: India to showcase efforts to combat the drug menace; meeting combating synthetic drugs, strengthening intelligence sharing and operational coordination

title: A majority of departments are yet to upload G.O.s on the Tamil Nadu government website
content: Only five departments have hosted their G.O.s issued in May and June on the website. The orders that are not available on the site pertain to the creation of Singappen Special Force and the provision of 100 additional units of electricity to domestic consumers who use 500 units bi-monthly

title: Pandavani legend Teejan Bai dies at 70
content: She was widely regarded as the foremost exponent of Pandavani, Chhattisgarh’s traditional folk art form that narrates episodes from the Indian epic Mahabharata

title: Police investigation stalled in Sai Krishna’s custodial death case
content: Special Investigation Team (SIT) officers are still in dark on the alleged custodial death and disappearance of Sai Krishna’s body; record the statements of the victim’s family members and the accused CI

title: Telangana releases draft CURE Bill for public consultation
content: Proposes new governance framework for the Hyderabad metropolitan region by replacing the decades-old Greater Hyderabad Municipal Corporation (GHMC) Act, 1955

title: Kaziranga bids adieu to iconic patrol elephant
content: Born in 1960, Joymala served the Kaziranga National Park and Tiger Reserve for 34 years since her induction as a ‘jungle warrior’ in 1992

title: CorroHealth lay-offs: Kerala Labour Minister to hold talks on July 10
content: Government is exploring every possible measure to protect the affected workers and timely actions have already been initiated since the issue came to light, says Minister

title: Jagan Mohan Reddy alleges misuse of police machinery in Andhra Pradesh
content: The YSRCP president accuses the ruling dispensation of encouraging political policing and undermining democratic values by intimidating critics instead of responding to public concerns

title: 'Have they house arrested her?' TMC cries 'super emergency' over Mamata security
content: Security forces surrounded Mamata Banerjee's residence as she prepared to visit Baruipur following a horrific crime. The TMC condemned the deployment, calling it a "super emergency" and questioning if their leader was being placed under house arrest. The party also criticized the BJP, alleging their promises of women's safety have proven hollow amidst the ongoing tragedy.

title: Stop child sex abuse ads on Insta at once: Govt to Meta
content: Government has issued a stern directive to Meta, demanding the immediate removal of all paid advertisements and content on Instagram promoting child sexual abuse material.  Authorities are demanding a detailed explanation within seven days regarding how such ads were permitted and what safeguards are being implemented to prevent future occurrences. This action follows reports of Instagram ads being used to direct users to illegal content.

title: 15 of 20 fertiliser ships stuck at Hormuz set sail, relief for farmers
content: India's fertilizer stocks are set to receive a significant boost as 15 out of 20 ships carrying vital cargo have successfully navigated the Strait of Hormuz. This, coupled with three months of exceeding domestic urea production targets, ensures ample supply for farmers during the crucial kharif season. The government's proactive approach in securing alternative routes and fostering international partnerships has strengthened the nation's fertilizer availability.

title: Australia chase record target to clinch seventh T20 World Cup
content: Chasing 151, the highest target ever set in a Women's T20 World Cup final, Australia reached 153/3 in 17.1 overs, finishing the job with 17 balls to spare.

title: First 25 mn US kids signing up for Trump Accounts to get personal donation from Dell couple
content: Dell Technologies founder and CEO Michael Dell and his wife Susan announced on July 4 that they'll give $250 each to the first 25 million qualifying American children who sign up for a Trump Account, the new government-backed investment vehicle that launched the same day. The pledge builds on the Dells' earlier $6.25 billion commitment. Here's how Trump Accounts work, who qualifies for the Dell donation, and what the money can eventually be used for.

title: India to double US gas buys, cut Gulf reliance
content: India is significantly boosting LPG imports from the US, aiming to double current purchases and reduce reliance on Gulf nations. This strategic shift, spurred by recent West Asian conflicts, ensures a diversified supply chain and aids in building a crucial 30-day strategic reserve.  The move also strengthens energy ties with the US, helping to balance trade.

title: Childcare, housework keep 69% urban women out of work: NSO
content: A recent National Statistics Office survey reveals that childcare and household duties sideline nearly 69% of women in India's largest cities from the workforce, underscoring a persistent gender bias in caregiving responsibilities. While women earn 23% less than men in these urban centers, the quality of their employment shows improvement compared to smaller towns.  The survey also highlights variations in female labor participation across different cities.

title: After 8,000 layoffs, Zuckerberg admits Meta misjudged AI progress
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg acknowledged internal restructuring hasn't yielded expected results, particularly in AI agent development, admitting executives 'miscalculated' timelines. Despite significant layoffs and AI investments, progress has been slower than anticipated. However, Zuckerberg anticipates substantial AI benefits within three to six months. Separately, the company addressed mouse-tracking software, assuring no employee data was used for AI training and confirming future opt-in policies.

title: Seaplane carrying 8 crashes into New York's East River, all rescued - video
content: A seaplane carrying eight people crashed into New York City's East River on Sunday, prompting a swift emergency response.  All passengers were rescued by the FDNY, with two sustaining minor injuries. The aircraft, which partially capsized after a hard landing, was later righted and towed. Authorities are investigating the incident, which follows a similar seaplane rescue last month.

title: More crude ahead as OPEC+ hikes August output by 188,000 barrels per day
content: OPEC+ has approved an additional 188,000 barrels per day production increase for August, aiming to boost global crude availability and ease price pressures, particularly for importing nations like India. This move, part of a broader unwinding of production cuts, comes as global benchmark Brent crude nears pre-conflict levels. While oil firms anticipate breaking even on petrol and diesel soon, cooking gas sales continue to incur losses.

title: Nanobubbles tested at Lincoln Memorial pool to clean water and fight algae
content: Microscopic nanobubbles are being tested at Washington D.C.'s Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool to combat algal blooms. This innovative technology, injecting ozone nanobubbles, effectively cleans water and holds promise for restoring oxygen-starved lakes and seas suffering from eutrophication. Scientists are exploring methods to deliver these oxygen-carrying bubbles directly to the sediment layer, where critical environmental issues begin, offering a potential solution for vast polluted water bodies.

title: China's Agibot completes world's first livestream of humanoid robots inspecting full production line
content: Chinese firm Agibot has showcased the world's first live broadcast of humanoid robots on a tablet production line. Eight Agibot G2 robots worked non-stop for six days at Longcheer Technology, successfully inspecting and sorting 17,625 tablets with a near-perfect success rate. This demonstration highlights the practical application of embodied AI in real-world industrial settings, working alongside human employees and existing machinery.

title: Syama Prasad Mookerjee devoted his life to India's unity and progress: PM Modi
content: Remembering Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee on his 125th birth anniversary, this tribute highlights his unwavering commitment to India's unity and selfless service. From his early life of privilege to his impactful roles as an educationist, minister, and founder of the Jana Sangh, Mookerjee championed national integrity and democratic values. His sacrifice for Jammu & Kashmir's integration remains a pivotal moment in India's history.

title: Just 28% NGOs, associations ever registered under FCRA currently active
content: A significant majority of NGOs registered under the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Act, 2010, are no longer active, with over 72% having their licenses cancelled or expired. Since 2012, out of 52,159 granted licenses, only 14,455 currently hold active status, enabling them to receive foreign funding. This tightening of regulations aims to enhance accountability and transparency among these organizations.

title: Egypt's 22,000-acre 'Octagon' pips Pentagon as world's largest defence headquarters
content: Egypt has unveiled its colossal new State Strategic Command headquarters, dubbed "Octagon," dwarfing the US Pentagon in size. This sprawling complex, covering approximately 22,000 acres, will centralize Egypt's military leadership, operations, and intelligence. President El Sisi inaugurated the "towering national edifice," highlighting its advanced technological infrastructure designed to manage military situations and confront national challenges. The Octagon represents a significant advancement in Egypt's command and control capabilities.

title: Why Harrison Butker was reportedly not invited to Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift's wedding
content: Controversial comments by Kansas City Chiefs placekicker Harrison Butker regarding women’s roles and other social issues had sparked widespread debate in 2024. While teammates like Patrick Mahomes expressed disagreement but acknowledged Butker as a person, Travis Kelce defended him as a good teammate, though not endorsing his views back then. Amidst this, a viral rumor suggests Butker was reportedly uninvited to Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift’s royal wedding. Despite most of the team receiving invitations from their star tight end.

title: Brittany Mahomes reveals Patrick Mahomes inspired an emotional promise to kids
content: Brittany Mahomes says becoming a mother has made her more ambitious and intentional about pursuing her goals. She hopes her children, Sterling, Bronze, and Golden, see both her and Patrick Mahomes working hard and following their dreams. Brittany believes leading by example will teach them that success comes through determination and perseverance. She also shared that her daughter Sterling has shown growing interest in her work, making those moments even more meaningful.

title: Lauren Kyle McDavid reacts to Darnell Nurse trade with heartfelt message
content: Lauren Kyle McDavid posted an emotional goodbye to Mikayla Nurse after Darnell Nurse left the Edmonton Oilers. The message touched on years of friendship between the families and provided a glimpse into the personal side of NHL trades, showing how offseason moves impact relationships beyond the rink and locker room.

title: Driving test for repeat offenders, interim compensation for crash victims on cards
content: Motorists with traffic violations will soon face mandatory driving tests for license renewal. The government also plans to empower tribunals to grant interim compensation to accident victims, speeding up relief. These proposed amendments to the Motor Vehicles Act, approved by a Group of Ministers, aim to curb errant drivers and enhance road safety. The bill is expected to be introduced in Parliament soon.

title: Enforce 'zero coal leakage' plan in Jharkhand: Amit Shah to CISF
content: Home Minister Amit Shah has ordered the CISF and coal ministry to establish rapid response teams and implement a "zero coal leakage plan" to combat rising illegal mining and coal theft in Jharkhand.  Multi-layered security will be deployed in vulnerable zones, with agencies now empowered to file cases, conduct searches, and seize illegally mined coal and equipment.  Shah stressed rigorous, coordinated action and regular reviews.

title: Army conducts mock drill in J&K to enhance operational preparedness
content: Indian Army conducted a large-scale mock drill in Jammu and Kashmir's Poonch district, simulating a terrorist attack to bolster operational readiness. The exercise, involving Rashtriya Rifles and other units along the LoC and hinterland, tested evacuation, safety protocols, and inter-agency coordination. This comes as heightened security measures are in place in Surankote and Mendhar following intelligence of potential infiltration and terror activities.

title: 6 killed, 1 injured as multi-storey 'chawl' collapses in Mumbai amid heavy rain; rescue ops on
content: Mumbai was battered by heavy rains on Sunday, leading to a multi-storey chawl collapse in Mankhurd that claimed at least six lives. The downpour also caused flight disruptions at the international airport and triggered multiple treefall incidents across the city, including one fatality in Kurla. The India Meteorological Department had issued a red alert, with several areas recording over 200 mm of rainfall.

title: E20 debate explained: Mileage loss, engine concerns and what's next
content: Automobile majors are backing the government's mandatory 20% ethanol blending in petrol (E20), despite consumer concerns about mileage drops and potential material degradation in older vehicles. While official studies suggest minimal impact, vehicle owners report noticeable fuel efficiency losses. The controversy intensifies as India achieved its E20 target early, with speculation rising about even higher blending levels, prompting calls for consumer choice regarding fuel options.

title: 'I challenge the darkness': Diljit Dosanjh reacts after 'Satluj' OTT removal
content: Diljit Dosanjh's film 'Satluj', previously facing CBFC issues, was removed from Zee5 in India just two days after its digital release. The actor reacted strongly, posting '#ichallengethedarkness' and stating the film's unavailability is a setback. The streaming platform expressed commitment to bringing the film back, emphasizing its authentic narrative, while acknowledging the pause in its availability.

title: History! Novak Djokovic breaks Roger Federer's all-time Wimbledon record
content: Novak Djokovic has etched his name further in Wimbledon history, becoming the men's singles player with the most match wins at the prestigious tournament, surpassing Roger Federer's record. Despite a challenging match and some on-court frustrations, Djokovic secured his quarterfinal berth. Meanwhile, the women's singles will see a new champion crowned as defending titleholder Barbora Krejcikova was eliminated.

title: Aamir's silver anklet steals show at ivory-themed wedding with Gauri Spratt - video
content: Aamir Khan's recent wedding to Gauri Spratt saw him embrace tradition with an ivory dhoti-kurta, but it was his unexpected chunky silver anklet that stole the show, sparking widespread online discussion.  The intimate ceremony at his Bandra home was attended by close family and friends, including his children and Gauri's son, marking a joyous new chapter for the couple.

title: 'At least 13 withdrew': US pressured countries to skip Khamenei's funeral, says report
content: The United States reportedly waged a diplomatic campaign to dissuade nations from attending the funeral of Iran's former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Iranian media claims at least 13 countries either withdrew or reduced their delegations following US pressure, with warnings about potential cuts to development aid and implications for bilateral ties. The funeral ceremonies are ongoing, with Khamenei's burial scheduled for July 9.

title: Pregnant woman’s body exhumed after father alleges dowry murder, secret burial
content: A pregnant woman's body was exhumed in Muzaffarnagar following her father's allegations of dowry murder. The 28-year-old's family claims she was killed by her husband and in-laws over dowry demands and buried without informing authorities. Police have registered a case and sent the body for a post-mortem examination to investigate the disturbing claims.

title: 'Biological terrorism' in occupied Ukraine? Kyiv claims Russia risking deadly outbreak
content: Ukraine's intelligence agency has issued a concerning warning regarding Russian forces reportedly burying infected livestock improperly in occupied Kherson. Kyiv asserts that these burial sites, especially those located near populated regions and groundwater sources, present a significant risk of anthrax. The Defence Intelligence of Ukraine cautions about the potential for biological terrorism and speculates on a possible 'false-flag' operation by Russia to frame Ukraine for any biological weapons incidents.

title: Don't rest Sooryavanshi after debut: Ex-cricketer sends clear message to Team India
content: Sooryavanshi became India's youngest international cricketer when he made his debut in the second T20I against England in Manchester on Saturday at the age of 15 years and 99 days.

title: Lock Upp Season 2 latest episode: Sunita Ahuja breaks down over food; wants to quit show
content: Govinda's wife, Sunita Ahuja, expressed deep regret over joining Lock Upp season 2, breaking down and lashing out at the makers for poor food quality. She refused to participate in tasks and expressed a strong desire to leave the show, citing her husband's initial reservations. Sunita's emotional plea was met with comfort from fellow contestants and a promise of discussion from host Riteish Deshmukh.

title: 'Catch the thieves, not critics': Congress slams VHP over Ayodhya temple donation probe letter
content: The Congress has accused the VHP and BJP of attempting to deflect from the Ayodhya Ram Temple donation theft investigation.  Following VHP president Alok Kumar's request for opposition leaders to provide evidence, Congress leader Pawan Khera questioned why the onus was on accusers instead of investigators. He alleged the real motive is to emotionally exploit Hindus rather than find culprits.

title: 'Free hits made it easier': Kishan points to Bishnoi's nightmare over after Eng loss
content: The defeat extended India's winless run in T20Is to four matches. India had lost both games against Ireland before the opening match against England was washed out and then suffered another defeat on Saturday.

title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s no-gift wedding policy explained
content: Modern couples are increasingly opting for 'no gift' policies, encouraging guests to contribute to honeymoon funds or specific experiences instead of traditional presents. While some find cash gifts impersonal, many see them as practical, especially when couples have already covered major expenses like honeymoons. Cultural expectations and personal finances significantly influence gift amounts, with contributions ranging from £50 to £400 or more, reflecting a shift in wedding gift traditions.

title: Mumbai rain havoc: Schools and colleges shut on Monday; flights hit, roads flooded
content: The weather forced the cancellation of four IndiGo flights and the diversion of 13 incoming aircraft operated by different airlines. All the diverted flights later returned to Mumbai and landed safely after conditions improved, according to sources. The airport operator said the suspension was necessary to ensure operational safety. According to sources, the cancelled IndiGo flights were 6E 395 from Delhi to Mumbai, 6E 5273 from Mumbai to Indore, 6E 552 from Indore to Mumbai and 6E 6613 from Mumbai to Delhi.

title: Who is 'real' TMC? Mamata and rebel camp to face crucial EC test on Monday
content: A significant political battle is unfolding within the Trinamool Congress as rival factions, led by Mamata Banerjee and Ritabrata Banerjee, prepare to present their claims to the Election Commission. The dispute centers on control of the party's name, symbol, and assets, escalating after a legislative revolt and the rebel group's takeover of the party headquarters. The EC's decision will determine the 'real' Trinamool Congress.

title: Heartwarming scenes! England captain carries son in her arms during national anthem
content: England captain Nat Sciver-Brunt captured hearts at the ICC Women's T20 World Cup 2026 final by walking onto the Lord's pitch with her infant son, Theo. This touching moment, witnessed by his wife Katherine Sciver-Brunt, added profound meaning to the match. Earlier, Sciver-Brunt had celebrated a century by rocking her bat like a baby, a gesture now amplified by this family-centric occasion.

title: After Sooryavanshi's debut, BCCI vice-president sends strong message to critics
content: BCCI vice-president Rajiv Shukla has lauded Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's India debut, emphasizing selection based on merit amidst social media speculation. The 15-year-old Bihar batter became the youngest Indian international cricketer, marking a historic moment. Despite his promising start, England secured a four-wicket victory in the second T20I, taking a 1-0 series lead after the first match was abandoned.

title: 'Khakis and cowardice': White supremacist march crashes America's birthday party
content: On America's 250th birthday, around 400 white nationalists from Patriot Front marched through Washington in matching attire.  The group, known for its white ethnonationalist views, staged a choreographed demonstration. While many citizens expressed disgust, the administration's response was muted, with the Interior Secretary citing free speech. President Trump, who spoke elsewhere, did not address the march, echoing his past comments on similar events.

title: Newlywed woman dies after fall from third floor in Delhi; family alleges dowry harassment, murder
content: A 28-year-old newlywed woman, Akriti, died after allegedly falling from the third floor of a Delhi residential complex. Her family has accused her husband and in-laws of dowry harassment and murder, rejecting the possibility of suicide. The marriage, a love-cum-arranged alliance, took place just two-and-a-half months ago. Police are investigating the incident, recording statements from family and witnesses.

title: 'Shocked, Saddened': Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj Ban Triggers Political Row
content: The Shiromani Gurdwara Parbandhak Committee (SGPC), the body that manages prominent gurdwaras in the country, and the Shiromani Akali Dal have thrown their weight behind the movie.

title: Zelesky Says Fight Continuing In Key Town That Kremlin Says It Seized
content: Moscow earlier this week announced it had taken the key eastern town of Kostyantynivka but Kyiv has denied that as a "lie", saying that it is defending the town.

title: JD Vance's US "Only Ally" Remark, Netanyahu's "1.4 Billion Indians" Reply
content: Israeli Prime Minister Bejamin Netanyahu has pushed back against JD Vance's comments, saying that his country gets "tremendous" support from India.

title: Trump To Meet Zelesnsky, Syrian President At NATO Summit In Turkey
content: Trump's meeting with Volodymyr Zelensky in Ankara comes amid growing efforts to end the invasion of Ukraine that Russia launched nearly four-and-a-half years ago.

title: Meghalaya's South Garo Hills Reports Zero Maternal Deaths For Eight Months
content: Despite difficult terrain, scattered villages and limited healthcare infrastructure, the district has achieved an estimated 83 per cent reduction in its Maternal Mortality Ratio (MMR).

title: 5 Feared Trapped As Chawl Collapses In Mumbai Amid Rain
content: Four to five persons are feared trapped after a ground-plus-three-storey 'chawl' partially collapsed in Mumbai's Mankhurd area on Sunday night amid heavy rains, civic officials said.

title: 'Abandoned Ram's Path': BJP Retort On Uddhav Thackeray's Temple Theft Barbs
content: Addressing a rally outside the Hanuman temple in Mumbai, Thackeray recited "Hanuman Stotra", "Hanuman Chalisa" and "Ram Raksha Stotra", and attacked the BJP over the Ram Temple theft.

title: In "Businesslike" Putin Call, Trump Offers To Help End Ukraine War, Again
content: The call, their fourth this year, took place just before a significant NATO summit in Turkey, due to begin on Tuesday and expected to be attended by Trump.

title: Iran's "No History" Reply To Trump's "One Shot" Threat Amid Khamenei Funeral
content: Iran's reaction followed a remark made by Trump, who claimed that the US has the capabilities to take out Iran's surviving top leadership "all in one shot" as they gathered to mourn late Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

title: 2 Months After Wedding, Delhi Woman Found Dead, Family Alleges Murder
content: Aakriti's family alleged that she has been was murdered. They accused her husband and in-laws of dowry harassment and physical abuse.

title: Japan Space Probe Performs Flyby Of Asteroid. It May Help Protect The Earth
content: Moving at a speed of more than 18,000 kilometres per hour, Hayabusa2 was not intended to collide with Torifune.

title: Delhi Records Its Warmest July Day In 2 Years
content: The national capital witnessed moderate rainfall during the afternoon, with Chhatarpur receiving 49 mm till 2.30 pm, the highest in Delhi-NCR.

title: Heavy Rain Causes Waterlogging, Traffic Congestion In Gurugram
content: The rainfall in Gurugram on Sunday inundated several areas of the city, causing traffic congestion, officials said.

title: 8, Including 4 Children, Injured In New York Shooting On US Independence Day
content: One of the victims, a 21-year-old woman, was in critical condition while the others were described as being stable and expected to survive, police said.

title: 2 Women Injured As Tree Crashes Down In Mumbai, Third Collapse In 6 Days
content: Local residents rushed to help as soon as the tree fell, working to free the two women trapped beneath it.

title: 'Millionaire' On Matrimonial Site Rapes Thane Woman, Dupes Her Of Rs 41 Lakh
content: A 38-year-old man from Maharashtra's Nandurbar has been booked for allegedly raping a woman and defrauding her of Rs 41.25 lakh after posing as a millionaire businessman on a matrimonial website, police said on Sunday.

title: Ticking Timebomb: Ukraine Says Russia Burying "Bioweapon" In Its Territory
content: Ukraine's Ministry of Defence says the problem is concentrated in the Kherson region, where it counts around fifty of these burial grounds. Roughly ten are considered particularly dangerous, including sites near Askania-Nova, Skadovsk and Zalizny Port.

title: 6 Fishermen Missing After Boat Goes Off Grid At Sea In Visakhapatnam
content: Family members said the fishermen called home on the afternoon of July 4, informing that they had started returning and expected to reach the harbour within about an hour. However, the boat never arrived.

title: Boy, 11, To Face Trial For Shopping Plaza Fire Which Killed 72 In Pakistan
content: An 11-year-old boy will stand trial after being named as an accused in the chargesheet filed by police related to the fire incident that killed 72 people at a shopping complex in Pakistan's Karachi in January.

title: Children’s film ignored at Kerala State Film Awards finds audience in OTT
content: School Chale Hum, a Malayalam children's film with fantasy elements, is  the second feature film by Merchant Navy officers Sreekanth E.G. and Adith U.S.

title: Pakistan air force captain gunned down in broad daylight while rescuing woman
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. title: Foundation laid for Rs 2,500-crore defence plant in Madhya Pradesh
content:
Madhya Pradesh CM Mohan Yadav

NEW DELHI: In a boost to defence manufacturing in Madhya Pradesh, CM Mohan Yadav Sunday laid the foundation stone for the Rs 2,500 crore missile manufacturing facility of Adani Defence and Aerospace in Shivpuri. The project will bridge a long-standing gap in India's defence supply chain by bringing system integration together with production of critical materials, enabling Indian armed forces to induct indigenous missile systems at scale, Adani Defence and Aerospace said in a statement. Speaking at the programme, Yadav said, "Shivpuri is advancing towards industrial and economic development. The establishment of the plant will bring large-scale investment to the region, generate new employment opportunities and position MP among the leading states in the country in the field of defence manufacturing."

End of Article
